2603.17208.title: SYMDIREC: A Neuro-Symbolic Divide-Retrieve-Conquer Framework for Enhanced RTL Synthesis and Summarization
2603.17208.paper_summary: Register-Transfer Level (RTL) synthesis and summarization are central to hardware design automation but remain challenging for Large Language Models (LLMs) due to rigid HDL syntax, limited supervision, and weak alignment with natural language. Existing prompting and retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) methods have not incorporated symbolic planning, limiting their structural precision. We introduce SYMDIREC, a neuro-symbolic framework that decomposes RTL tasks into symbolic subgoals, retrieves relevant code via a fine-tuned retriever, and assembles verified outputs through LLM reasoning. Supporting both Verilog and VHDL without LLM fine-tuning, SYMDIREC achieves ~20% higher Pass@1 rates for synthesis and 15-20% ROUGE-L improvements for summarization over prompting and RAG baselines, demonstrating the benefits of symbolic guidance in RTL tasks.
2603.17208.paper_summary_zh: 註冊傳輸級 (RTL) 合成和摘要對於硬體設計自動化至關重要，但由於嚴格的 HDL 語法、有限的監督和與自然語言的弱對齊，對於大型語言模型 (LLMs) 來說仍然具有挑戰性。現有的提示和檢索增強生成 (RAG) 方法未納入符號規劃，限制了其結構精確性。我們介紹 SYMDIREC，一個神經符號框架，將 RTL 任務分解為符號子目標，通過微調的檢索器檢索相關代碼，並通過 LLM 推理組裝經過驗證的輸出。SYMDIREC 支援 Verilog 和 VHDL，無需 LLM 微調，對於合成達到約 20% 的 Pass@1 提高，對於摘要則在提示和 RAG 基準上實現 15-20% 的 ROUGE-L 改進，展示了符號指導在 RTL 任務中的好處。
2603.17208.author: Prashanth Vijayaraghavan et.al.
2603.17208.authors: Prashanth Vijayaraghavan, Apoorva Nitsure, Luyao Shi, Charles Mackin, Ashutosh Jadhav, David Beymer, Ehsan Degan, Vandana Mukherjee
